result:
I’d let every person truly understand—viscerally, not abstractly—how another person feels when they are hurt. Not mere sympathy or intellectual empathy, but the full, unshielded weight of their pain laid inside your own nervous system for a moment longer than is comfortable. A built-in mirror response appearing every single time one mind can diminish or damage another.

The change would be instantaneous and irrevocable: war rooms would empty themselves the moment a commander felt the terror of the first incoming shell, assembly lines for weapons would halt once the machinist experienced each round as it enters a stranger’s lung, billionaires’ hearts would race with the panic of a tenant evicted at midnight in winter. Cruelty would become instantly self-punishing; privilege would carry an allergic reaction.

The world would still have conflict—ambitions, disagreements, scarcity—but it would be stripped of the possibility of anonymous distance. To harm someone else would always mean to sit sweating inside their wound. Overnight, cruelty becomes the most undesirable currency on Earth.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
